channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Heaviest snowfall in a decade in some areas as winter storm threatens US
item.description: MISSION, Kan. (AP) — A blast of snow, ice, wind and plunging temperatures stirred up dangerous travel conditions in parts of the central U.S. on Sunday, as a disruptive winter storm brought the possibility of the “heaviest snowfall in a decade” to some areas.
item.dc:creator: HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH and BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Heavy snow brings widespread disruption across the UK and Germany
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Heavy snow and freezing rain brought widespread disruption across Europe on Sunday, particularly in the U.K. and Germany, with several major airports forced to suspend flights. With the weather set to stay inclement on Sunday in the U.K.
item.dc:creator: PAN PYLAS and MONIKA SCISLOWSKA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Golden Globes: What to look for and how to watch on Sunday night
item.description: Hollywood is getting dressed up as the Golden Globes return for their annual champagne-soaked celebration of film and television that serves as the ceremonial start to awards season.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: 102-year-old veteran celebrates birthday at Hooters
item.description: Last year, Cerezo celebrated his 101st birthday at Hooters, and because he enjoyed the experience so much, he had his birthday party there again this year.
item.dc:creator: Dave Burge, NewsNation

item.title: The Golden Globes are Sunday night. Here are five things to look for and how to watch them
item.description: After a rocky few years and the disbanding of the Hollywood Foreign Press Association, the Golden Globes have seemingly stabilized. Now the question is: Can they still put on a good show? The 82nd Golden Globes, beginning Sunday night at 8 p.m.
item.dc:creator: JAKE COYLE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Arizona death row inmate wants to be executed earlier than the state is planning
item.description: An Arizona death row inmate called on the state Supreme Court to skip legal formalities and carry out his death sentence earlier than officials were planning.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Drawn to New Orleans' iconic street of celebration, a night of partying becomes a nightmare
item.description: The night started out ripe for celebrating the new year as the party pulsing down Bourbon Street in New Orleans drew revelers from near and far.
item.dc:creator: Adam Geller, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Jimmy Carter's state funeral has started. Here's what to know
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Six days of funeral observances for former President Jimmy Carter began Saturday in Georgia, where he died on Dec. 29 at the age of 100.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Washington Post cartoonist quits after paper rejects sketch of Bezos bowing to Trump
item.description: A cartoonist has decided to quit her job at the Washington Post after an editor rejected her sketch of the newspaper's owner and other media executives bowing before President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Indie filmmaker Jeff Baena, Aubrey Plaza's husband, found dead at Los Angeles home
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Writer-director Jeff Baena, whose darkly comedic independent films included “The Little Hours” and who was married to his frequent creative collaborator Aubrey Plaza, has died. He was 47.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Royal family pays tribute to Prince William, Harry's nanny who lost stepson in New Orleans attack
item.description: Prince William shared his condolences to the family of his former nanny after her stepson was killed in the New Year’s terror attack in New Orleans.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, WGN 9

item.title: Hundreds of animals killed in Dallas shopping center fire
item.description: A fire that broke out at a shopping center in Dallas on Friday morning killed more than 500 animals, most of which were small birds, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 39,000 John Deere mowers recalled
item.description: About 39,000 John Deere ZTrak™ Zero Turn Mowers with Kawasaki Engines have been recalled because the voltage regulator inside the mower's engine can fail while in use or while the product is being stored.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hillary Clinton, George Soros and Denzel Washington will receive the highest US civilian honor
item.description: Former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton, Democratic philanthropist George Soros and actor-director Denzel Washington will be awarded the nation's highest civilian honor on Saturday in a White House ceremony.
item.dc:creator: KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Take these steps to protect yourself from winter weather dangers
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — Winter weather brings various hazards that people have to contend with to keep warm and safe. These dangers can include carbon monoxide poisoning, hypothermia and frozen pipes that can burst and make homes unlivable.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO and PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida mom makes teen son turn himself in for crime
item.description: A 15-year-old boy accused of armed attempted robbery was brought to Florida deputies to be turned in, by his own mother.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WGN 9

item.title: Jimmy Carter's funeral begins by tracing 100 years from rural Georgia to the world stage
item.description: Jimmy Carter’s long public goodbye began in Georgia, where the 39th U.S. president’s life started more than 100 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Kate Payne And Bill Barrow, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Suspected illegal immigrant's fake bomb threat shuts down SC highway
item.description: A suspected illegal immigrant was arrested in South Carolina after officials say he made a fake bomb threat during a traffic stop which ultimately led to a section of the I-85 being shut down for nearly five hours.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Congresswoman calls for Fort Liberty whistleblowers to come forward after NOLA, Vegas attacks
item.description: Florida Rep. Anna Paulina Luna is urging anyone who encountered the New Orleans terrorist or Las Vegas bomber at Fort Liberty, formerly Fort Bragg, to come forward.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Craziest air travel moments of 2024
item.description: The year 2024 was filled with numerous incidents aboard airplanes from crashes and fights to liquids making their way from the bathroom through the aisle.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sunday school class with Jimmy Carter: What it was like
item.description: PLAINS, Ga. (AP) — It never got old. No matter how many times one crammed into the modest sanctuary at Maranatha Baptist Church, there was always some wisdom to be gleaned from the measured, Bible-inspired words of Jimmy Carter.
item.dc:creator: PAUL NEWBERRY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: BBB provides tips on buying Detroit Lions tickets, avoiding scams
item.description: The Better Business Bureau of West Michigan is providing some tips if you’re looking to buy tickets to the Detroit Lions game on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Meghan Bunchman, NewsNation

item.title: Assumption University students charged in 'To Catch a Predator' TikTok scheme
item.description: Five Assumption College students are facing charges after allegedly luring a man to campus, framing him as a sexual predator and then attacking him, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bawden, NewsNation

item.title: Blinken heads to Asia, Europe on last expected trip as top US diplomat
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Secretary of State Antony Blinken will embark on what is expected to be his final overseas trip in office this weekend, traveling to South Korea, Japan and France. The State Department announced Friday that Blinken would visit Seoul, Tokyo and Paris beginning Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: In-N-Out Burger is expanding again in 2025. Here's where
item.description: Irvine, California-based In-N-Out Burger is planning to bring its mustard-grilled burgers and spiritual fountain sodas to new cities and states in the coming years, with a handful of those restaurants confirmed across three states in 2025.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: Former employee charged with child abuse after babies found with 'unexplainable fractures' at Henrico Doctors' Hospital’s NICU
item.description: A former employee of Henrico Doctors' Hospital’s neonatal intensive care unit (NICU) has been charged with malicious wounding and child abuse after an investigation into babies being found with "unexplainable fractures" at the NICU.
item.dc:creator: Katelyn Harlow, NewsNation

item.title: Ex-employee charged after babies found with 'unexplainable fractures' at Virginia hospital
item.description: The charges comes after the hospital said three babies were discovered with "unexplainable fractures" in late November and December of 2024. A similar incident took place the previous year.
item.dc:creator: Katelyn Harlow, ABC 4

item.title: Finalized tax credit for cleaner hydrogen gets a cautious OK from some environmental groups
item.description: The Biden administration released long-awaited final rules Friday for a tax credit that will send billions of dollars to producers of cleaner hydrogen.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ruidoso elk entangled with stool was freed
item.description: Elk becoming tangled in lawn furniture or other foreign objects has become a recurring issue in Ruidoso.
item.dc:creator: Jocelyn Flores, NewsNation

item.title: How to adjust your budget if you're unemployed
item.description: More than a quarter (26%) of households live "paycheck to paycheck," spending over 95% of their income on necessities.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KRON4

item.title: Watch a replay of the 2025 Rose Parade presented by Honda
item.description: LA's Very Own KTLA 5 is proud to offer this replay of the 136th Rose Parade presented by Honda from New Year’s Day, Jan. 1, 2025. The theme of the 136th Rose Parade was “Best Day Ever!” Billie Jean King, a sports icon and equality champion, was the parade's Grand Marshal.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, ABC 4
